description: This lecture discusses the role of voting in computer science, focusing on participatory budgeting and the elicitation of preferences from a community. It explores the complexities of voting systems, particularly in elections with multiple candidates, and introduces concepts like rank aggregation and crowdsourcing.
topic_summary: The lecture covers the intersection of voting and computer science, emphasizing how voting systems can be analyzed through the lens of incentives. It highlights rank aggregation as a method to combine different ranking algorithms and discusses crowdsourcing as a form of voting where workers provide input on tasks.
